objective: Wire presentation registry, shared component decision, and carrier-specific natural-language layout for the Tiny Brief Intake fixture.
implemented_capabilities: JSON loaders for presentation decisions, shared component patterns, natural-language layout, carrier adaptation, containment hierarchy, growth/completeness rules, z-axis layering, state placement, and Figma reconstruction metadata.
Presentation registry validation for duplicate IDs, equivalent-mode consistency, complete interaction message coverage, unknown message-ref rejection, and transient-only decision copy rejection.
Shared component registry validation for semantic reuse, visual-only reuse rejection, and layout pattern-ref binding.

Natural-language layout package validation for canonical prose authority, index-only inventory role, carrier coverage, containment depth, content growth, information completeness, required information ref binding, z-axis layering, state placement, surface reference integrity, Figma metadata, and cross-reference integrity.
Layout package validation materializes iterable rule inputs before multi-pass validation so generator callers cannot bypass reference integrity checks.
Tiny Brief presentation registry mapping every interaction message and recording validate/generate primary action treatment.
Tiny Brief shared component registry recording primary action, overlay status panel, and brief field stack patterns.
Tiny Brief carrier adaptation profile covering desktop, tablet, mobile, mobile iOS, and mobile Material constraints.

Tiny Brief layout artifacts covering containment hierarchy, height/width information access, required information refs, state placement, surface placement, and Material-compatible z-axis layering without adding product scope.

deferred_due_scope.reason: P2-IMPL-04 owns compiler input, semantic unit inventory, conservation report, and FINAL_DRD assembly after presentation/layout artifacts are approved.
Build lock is a separate lock step after implementation candidates and reviews are ready.
